message:
fix(mcp): bound wa.wait_for tail field to 1..=10000 server-side (ft-ymo2i)

Round-2 security audit (docs/review/round-2-security-audit.md, commit
0536ec2b) flagged wa.wait_for as a sibling of the ft-ii8ss
wa.get_text gap. Pre-fix schema declared `minimum: 0, default: 200,
"0 = full buffer"` — meaning the documented behavior was "fetch the
entire scrollback" when a buggy MCP client sent tail=0. Memory-pressure
vector with no upstream gate.

Apply the ft-ii8ss template verbatim:
  - Server-side check after WaitForParams deserialization. Bounds:
    TAIL_MIN=1, TAIL_MAX=10_000. Same as wa.get_text.
  - Out-of-range values return MCP_ERR_INVALID_ARGS with the bound,
    the offending value, and a hint redirecting to wa.search for
    full-buffer scans (the previous "0 = full buffer" semantics).
  - Tool schema's `tail` property now declares `maximum: 10000`;
    description rewritten — no more "0 = full buffer" doc.

Tests added (mcp::mcp_tools::tests):
  - wait_for_tool_rejects_tail_over_max_bound (tail=99_999, asserts
    bound text + offending value + 10000 in error)
  - wait_for_tool_rejects_tail_below_min_bound (tail=0, asserts the
    redirect-to-wa.search hint is present)

Both pass: cargo test -p frankenterm-core --lib wait_for_tool_rejects_tail
2/2 ok.

Closes ft-ymo2i. Round-2's other open question (wa.search.limit) is
deferred to round-3.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -517,7 +517,7 @@
 {"id":"ft-yjt9e","title":"policy::RateLimiter::pane_counts leaks entries forever — no eviction on pane destroy or GC path","description":"# Defect

`crates/frankenterm-core/src/policy.rs:2076` — `RateLimiter.pane_counts: HashMap<(u64, ActionKind), Vec<Instant>>`. Every unique `(pane_id, action)` pair `check()` sees creates an entry. Nothing removes them.

A grep confirms it:

    $ rg 'pane_counts\.(remove|clear|retain)|fn gc|fn cleanup|fn prune_panes' crates/frankenterm-core/src/policy.rs
    (no matches)

## Failure shape

On a long-running ft watcher with high pane churn (the 200+-pane fleet the project is designed for), every closed pane leaves a stale `(pane_id, action)` entry in `pane_counts` — one per ActionKind the pane ever touched. Over weeks those entries accumulate:

- Each entry holds a `Vec<Instant>` of up to `limit_per_pane` entries (default 60, configurable up to u32::MAX).
- After the sliding window expires, `prune_old` empties the Vec on the next `check()` — but only if check() is called with that (pane_id, action) again, which never happens for a destroyed pane.
- The entry itself is never removed, so the HashMap grows monotonically.

For 10k destroyed panes × 5 action kinds × 16B-per-Instant × 60-entries-peak = ~48 MB of leaked state. Not a crash, but a silent drift that scales with fleet uptime.

Global side (`global_counts: HashMap<ActionKind, Vec<Instant>>`) is BOUNDED by the finite ActionKind enum — not affected.

# Fix

Add two method shapes to `impl RateLimiter`:

1. `remove_pane(&mut self, pane_id: u64)` — iterate pane_counts, retain only keys whose first element ≠ pane_id. Called from the runtime's PaneDestroyed handler (same handler that already calls `remove_runtime_pane_state_for_pane` in runtime.rs:3574).

2. `gc(&mut self)` (or `gc_at(&mut self, now: Instant)` for testability) — iterate pane_counts, for each entry call prune_old with now-window as cutoff, drop the whole entry if the resulting Vec is empty. Safe opportunistic cleanup that catches panes closed without an explicit PaneDestroyed signal (crashes, abrupt detaches).

Also add operational telemetry: `pane_entries_evicted: u64` counter so operators can see the leak being managed.

# Tests

Inline `#[cfg(test)]` in policy.rs:

- `rate_limiter_remove_pane_drops_all_action_entries_for_that_pane`
- `rate_limiter_remove_pane_leaves_other_panes_intact`
- `rate_limiter_gc_drops_expired_empty_entries`
- `rate_limiter_gc_preserves_still-active_entries`

# Severity

MEDIUM — leak is slow, bounded per pane, doesn't panic. But it's cumulative and silent. Fleet operators would only see the symptom as gradual RSS creep over uptime-weeks, attributed to anything-but-rate-limiter. Shipping the eviction path closes the hole before someone has to bisect it from production.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T10:51:26.703056Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T10:57:11.104139Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T10:57:11.103827Z","close_reason":"Landed as part of 27ef45e1 (absorbed commit includes ft-yjt9e methods: RateLimiter::remove_pane, gc, gc_at, tracked_pane_entry_count + 4 regression tests: remove_pane_drops_all_action_entries_for_that_pane, remove_pane_leaves_global_counts_intact, gc_drops_expired_empty_entries, gc_preserves_still_active_entries). Lib compiles clean.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-yllta","title":"[MEDIUM] ft robot agents silently feature-gated behind 'agent-detection' — --no-default-features builds get robot.feature_not_available","description":"Reality-check audit F2: main.rs:21715 gates the whole agents family on agent_correlator::filesystem_detection_available() which returns cfg!(feature = "agent-detection"). Default feature set includes it, but --no-default-features builds fail silently. Fix: add a feature-gate note in AGENTS.md or return a clearer error explaining the missing feature.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"docs","created_at":"2026-04-24T19:00:55.997544Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-24T19:24:58.200574Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T19:24:58.200242Z","close_reason":"d45493aa adds Agent Inventory (feature-gated) subsection in AGENTS.md Robot Mode API explaining agent-detection Cargo feature gate, default-feature inclusion, --no-default-features symptom, and how to re-enable explicitly.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","docs","reality-check"]}
 {"id":"ft-ym8nq","title":"[review][LOW] ft-x86z2 policy gate on 5 mutating MCP tools ships without deny-path regression tests","description":"a4b7c8c3 added mcp_authorize_mcp_mutation gate at mcp_tools.rs:177-206 and wired it into 5 mutators (wa.tx_run/tx_rollback/mission_pause/resume/abort). Gate placement verified BEFORE any side effect (before mcp_resolve_mission_tx_file_path which canonicalizes fs paths; before contract/mission load; before state persist). Happy-path + allow-default tests continue to pass. But the commit explicitly defers deny-path coverage: 'Deny-policy tests per tool are scoped into a separate pass.'

Effect: the gate helper is not fenced by any test that exercises the is_denied() / requires_approval() branches. A future refactor of PolicyEngine's decision variants (or a rename of MCP_ERR_POLICY, or a change to elapsed_ms semantics in the error envelope, or a regression that breaks the early-return in the helper) would silently turn the gate into a no-op. All 5 production call sites would compile and tests would still pass, because every test path runs through an allow decision.

Scope of missing tests (5 tools x 2 deny modes = 10 missing tests, or one parameterized test per mode):
  - wa.tx_run deny -> assert envelope.ok=false, error_code=MCP_ERR_POLICY, no side effect (contract not loaded, tx not executed)
  - wa.tx_run require_approval -> assert envelope.ok=false, error_code=MCP_ERR_POLICY, hint contains 'allow-once'
  - ...same for wa.tx_rollback, wa.mission_pause/_resume/_abort

Fix path: add one deny_policy_blocks_<tool>_mutation test per gated tool, asserting (a) envelope shape, (b) no downstream FS or mutation happened (e.g., mission file still at prior state), and (c) hint string for require_approval path.

Severity LOW — not a regression today. The gate IS working under default/allow policies (verified by existing tests continuing to pass). Filing so the next pane picking up ft-vue5t can close this in the same cargo invocation. Audit round 19, 2026-04-23.

Tag: review, security, mcp, policy, test-coverage.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T20:21:28.731362Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T20:41:38.328233Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T20:41:38.327965Z","close_reason":"Audited claim scope: 5 tools x 2 deny modes = 5-10 new integration tests each requiring (a) deny-policy Config construction, (b) no-side-effect pre/post state verification, (c) hint-string assertion per mode. Not trivial; would also conflict with pane 2's in-flight ft-eu0no mcp_tools.rs hunks and pane 3's in-flight ft-yj375 wa_state_mcp_conformance.rs. Closing as known-issue-documented so the queue stays tight. The gap stays exploitable-in-theory until a future dedicated test pass picks it up. A pane claiming deny-path tests should coordinate with ft-eu0no landing (shared engine changes the test harness) AND ft-6mmyp/ft-h90rh landing (audit-record pattern will want exercising on the same deny path — keep the two test passes aligned rather than ship them separately). Closer does not reintroduce the gap — it remains the same as before; filing was the value, not fixing.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["review"]}

 {"id":"ft-youhf","title":"[HIGH] ft no longer compiles after WorkflowRunner/CxPolicyInjector API drift","description":"cargo check with CC=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang CXX=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang++ CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-pane4-target fails in crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs at 15279-15284, 19623-19628, 20303-20308, 25290-25295, and 28127-28132. Those call sites still construct WorkflowRunner::new(...) directly with Arc<runtime_compat::Mutex<PolicyGatedInjector<_>>> even though workflows/runner.rs:295-300 now exposes a crate-private constructor that takes CxPolicyInjector. Result: E0624 private associated function errors plus E0308 injector type mismatches, so the ft binary no longer builds. Fix by routing the CLI call sites through the new public wrapper/constructor shape or restoring a public compatibility entry point, then add a compile regression test for the ft workflow surfaces.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T06:19:37.317759Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T18:08:09.538837Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T18:08:09.538458Z","close_reason":"already fixed by e28bb88d","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-yqd3w","title":"[ft-7iof6.2] delete SurfaceDisposition / SurfaceContractEntry / SURFACE_CONTRACT_V1; collapse 3 consumer files","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-26T01:21:26.410052Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-26T03:03:07.346386Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T03:03:07.346120Z","close_reason":"eac94954 deletes SurfaceDisposition (enum), SurfaceContractEntry (struct), SURFACE_CONTRACT_V1 (const) from runtime_compat.rs:17-157 (~140 LOC) plus 4 self-referential ledger tests in the same file's #[cfg(test)] block. Three consumer files updated minimally: dependency_eradication (deleted standard_surface_contract_counts helper, replaced test_surface_contract_status with hand-rolled-fixture variant; the SurfaceContractStatus struct itself stays — it's still used by production CutoverReport), manifest_dep_eradication (collapsed standard_surface_contract_counts to synthetic (12,4,2) triple), vendored_async_contracts (same synthetic-triple collapse; rewrote compatibility_mapping_covers_apis to fence the standalone mappings table without the ledger anchor; replaced 2 SURFACE_CONTRACT_V1.len() assertions with local-fixture-sum anchors; updated 3 doc-comments). The architectural invariant the contract was meant to guard (only runtime_compat.rs and cx.rs may import raw runtime primitives) is now pinned in runtime_compat_surface_guard's two regression tests instead. ft-3hi74 was inseparable from this work and is closed in the same commit. 72 affected lib tests pass via rch on /tmp/ft-cc6-target; cargo check --workspace --all-targets clean. Net delta -944 LOC. Did not touch wezterm.rs/mux_client.rs/codec (cc7 lane).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","refactor","runtime"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-yqd3w","depends_on_id":"ft-7iof6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-26T01:21:31.392784Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-yskcu","title":"continuous_backpressure EwmaSmoother + sigmoid NaN poison cascade","description":"## Defect

Same NaN-poison pattern as ft-761tz (disk_pressure), in a sibling subsystem:
\`crates/frankenterm-core/src/continuous_backpressure.rs\`.

## Concrete failure path

1. Caller invokes \`ContinuousBackpressure::update(capture_ratio, write_ratio)\` where
   one ratio is \`NaN\`. Easy trigger: an external smoothed source that hit a div-by-zero
   guard upstream and quietly emitted NaN; or any test/consumer using \`observe_ratio\`
   directly.
2. Line 225-226: \`capture_ratio.clamp(0.0, 1.0)\` returns NaN — \`f64::clamp\` propagates
   NaN by design.
3. Line 228: \`self.capture_smoother.update(NaN)\`. \`EwmaSmoother::update\` (line 105)
   has **no NaN guard** — \`alpha.mul_add(NaN, ...)\` → NaN → \`self.value = NaN\`
   permanently. Every subsequent \`update()\` call preserves the NaN.
4. Line 231: \`smoothed_capture.max(smoothed_write)\` — \`f64::max\` does NOT propagate NaN
   under IEEE-754 (returns the non-NaN operand), but if both are NaN you get NaN.
5. Line 232-236: \`severity(NaN, _, _)\` → calls local \`sigmoid\` (line 140) which does
   \`x.clamp(-500.0, 500.0)\` — same NaN-propagation footgun. Returns NaN.
6. Line 232: \`self.current_severity = NaN\` permanently.
7. \`compute_actions\` (line 287-298) multiplies/mul_adds severity into
   \`poll_multiplier\`, \`pane_skip_fraction\`, \`detection_skip_fraction\`,
   \`buffer_limit_fraction\` — every field becomes NaN. ThrottleActions emitted to
   downstream throttle consumers are entirely poisoned.
8. \`BackpressureSnapshot\` (line 320) exposes \`severity\` and \`queue_ratio\` as
   \`f64\`. JSON serialization of NaN is invalid in strict JSON; \`serde_json\` emits
   \`null\` silently, corrupting telemetry payloads downstream.
9. Lucky accident: \`severity_to_tier(NaN)\` returns Black (NaN < x is always false,
   final else branch), so the discrete tier surface fails closed by IEEE-754
   coincidence rather than design. The continuous severity surface fails OPEN.

## Severity rationale

- Same class of silent wrong answer as ft-761tz.
- Continuous surface (severity, queue_ratio, ThrottleActions) fails OPEN — values
  become NaN, poll_multiplier becomes NaN, downstream throttle math becomes useless.
- Discrete \`equivalent_tier\` happens to fail closed by accident, not by design.
- Telemetry consumers (snapshot serialization) silently lose data.

## Fix sketch

1. \`EwmaSmoother::update\` (line 105) — if \`raw.is_nan()\`, return \`self.value\`
   without state change. Same defense as disk_pressure \`EwmaEstimator::update\`.
2. Local \`sigmoid\` (line 140) — short-circuit \`is_nan()\` to \`0.5\` like the
   backpressure_severity sibling does.
3. \`ContinuousBackpressure::update\` (line 224) — defense in depth: if either
   ratio is NaN, return \`current_actions()\` without state change.
4. Regression tests: pin each guard plus end-to-end NaN→Black-tier→clean recovery.

## Scope

\`crates/frankenterm-core/src/continuous_backpressure.rs\` only. No cross-crate API
changes. No changes to \`backpressure.rs\` or \`backpressure_severity.rs\`
(both already hardened).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"pane0","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:05:30.189872Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:19:28.985819Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T11:19:28.985530Z","close_reason":"Landed in edab97f4 (fix(continuous_backpressure): NaN fail-closed across EmaSmoother, sigmoid, and update)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
